name: branching-strategy-selection
description: Validate that the skill recommends an appropriate branching strategy based on team context, with clear rationale and actionable configuration steps.
input: Our team has 8 developers working on a B2B SaaS product. We deploy to production twice a week. We currently use GitFlow with develop, release, and hotfix branches, but PRs are taking 3-5 days to merge and we have frequent merge conflicts. We want to move faster. What branching strategy should we adopt and how do we migrate?

assertions.description: Must recommend trunk-based development or GitHub Flow for a team that wants to ship faster, not double down on GitFlow.
Must mention feature flags as the mechanism for handling incomplete work on main, since trunk-based development requires merging frequently.
Must emphasize short-lived branches (1-2 days) as the key practice change, since the current 3-5 day PR cycle is the root cause of merge conflicts.
Must provide a migration plan from GitFlow to the recommended model, not just describe the target state.
Must include branch protection configuration as part of the recommendation, since unprotected branches undermine any branching strategy.

name: commit-message-writing
description: Validate that the skill produces properly formatted conventional commit messages with meaningful descriptions that explain intent.
input:
I made the following changes and need help writing commit messages:
1. Added rate limiting middleware to the Express API routes (max 100 requests per minute per IP) to prevent abuse after we saw a spike in traffic last week.
2. Fixed a bug where the user profile page crashed when the user had no avatar URL (it was passing null to the Image component).
3. Refactored the database connection pool to use a singleton pattern instead of creating a new pool per request.
4. Updated the CI pipeline to run tests in parallel across 4 workers instead of sequentially.
Write conventional commit messages for each change.

assertions.description: The rate limiting middleware is a new feature and must use the feat type, not fix or chore.
The avatar null crash is a bug fix and must use the fix type with a clear description of what was broken.
The database connection pool change is a refactor (no behavior change) and must use the refactor type.
The pipeline parallelization is a CI change and must use the ci type, not build or chore.
Must use or explain imperative mood in commit subjects (add, fix, refactor) rather than past tense (added, fixed, refactored).
